title: Offline Lab package.yaml
description: Build-time package definition. Written by the app author, read by buildctl. Fields not set here fall back to Docker image labels, then to defaults where applicable. See docs/specs/package-format.md.

spec_version.description: Schema version. Always '1' for this version of the spec.

name.description: Package name. Lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only. Must start with alphanumeric.

version.description: Package version. Must be valid semver.

arch.description: Target architecture. Maps to a Docker platform string at build time (e.g. arm64 → linux/arm64, i386 → linux/386).

homepage.description: Package or upstream project homepage URL.

tags.description: Classification tags used for repository search and filtering.

security_contact.description: Email address or URL for vulnerability disclosure.

sbom_url.description: URL to a published SBOM for this package. Null if not provided.

backend.description: Build backend used to produce the filesystem. 'docker' (default) uses docker buildx. See docs/specs/build.md.

backend_arguments.description: Backend-specific build arguments. Valid keys depend on the selected backend. Per-backend keys are defined when each backend is documented. See docs/specs/build.md.

systemd_profile.description: systemd portable service security profile. 'custom' shifts full security responsibility to the app author — flagged in appctl list and the repo index.

socket_activation.description: If true, the service uses socket activation. A .socket unit must exist inside the squashfs.

config.description: Namespace path for the config volume (e.g. /etc/mosquitto).

data.description: Namespace path for the data volume (e.g. /var/lib/mosquitto).

volumes.description: Persistent volume mappings. At most two keys: config and data. Apps log via stderr/stdout to the systemd journal — no log volume.

ports.description: Network ports the service listens on.

devices.description: Hardware devices the service requires or optionally uses.

resources.description: Expected resource usage at three load levels. Optional — if absent, appctl skips the resource check with a warning.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle hook unit names. Omit the entire block if the app has no hooks. If present, each defined key must be a non-empty unit name string.

strip.description: Binary stripping configuration. Build-time only — not included in the generated metadata JSON.

expose.description: If true, an iptables accept rule is applied for this port on install.

required.description: If false, the service starts even if the device is absent.

cpu_percent.description: Sustained CPU usage as a percentage of one core.

storage_mb.description: Disk space in MB (squashfs + data dir combined).

pre_start.description: Unit name to run after portablectl attach, before enable and first start.

post_start.description: Unit name to run after the service is running for the first time.

pre_update.description: Unit name to run after new image is attached, before service restart.

post_update.description: Unit name to run after the service is running on the new image.

pre_remove.description: Unit name to run before the service is stopped and the image is detached.

enabled.description: If true, unused shared libraries are removed after build. Libraries loaded via dlopen are invisible to ldd — declare them in keep[].

keep.description: Paths inside the rootfs to preserve during stripping.
